meta.title: Toggle / Radio / Checkbox
meta.description: Selection controls — Checkbox for multi-select, Radio Button for exclusive choice, and Toggle Switch for binary state.

hero.title: Selection Controls
hero.desc: Selection controls help users pick options. The family splits into Checkbox for multi-select, Radio Button for exclusive single-select, and Toggle Switch for on/off binary state.

components.title: Components
components.desc: Selection Control includes Checkbox (one or more, multi-select), Radio Button (one of many, single-select), and Toggle Switch (immediate on/off state change). Apply the right control for each use case.

group.title: Selection Control Group
group.desc: Selection controls also group as multiples within a single purpose. Within the group, controls form either mutually exclusive or independent relationships.

checkbox.title: Checkboxes Group
checkbox.desc: Groups single or multi-selectable items together as a selection set. Most commonly used for multi-filter purposes.
checkbox.example: (e.g. Statistics and Defects Side Filter, Task Settings)

radio.title: Radio Buttons Group
radio.desc: Used when only one option may be selected from many. Currently not used inside Zoomable Wind.

toggle.title: Toggle-Switches Group
toggle.desc: Toggle Switches already act as Checked / Unchecked (On/Off) by themselves — typically used singly. When grouped, place toggles serving similar roles together, each with its own Label.

checkbox.title: Checkbox
checkbox.desc: Checkbox is used when there are one or more independent options, and the user may select none, one, or many. When standalone, a Checkbox can act as a feature on/off toggle.

state.title: State
state.desc: Checkbox states split by Checked / Unchecked into Default, Active, Disabled. Inside Selection Control, Active behaves as Hover — the user-facing rest states are Default and Disabled.

size.title: Size
size.desc: Four sizes — Large, Medium, Small, Xsmall. The default is 16 × 16. Pick sizes to create hierarchy between elements when needed.

spacing.title: Spacing
spacing.text: Checkbox-to-label margin is 8px at Large, Medium, sMedium; 4px at Small. List items use 12px padding to avoid mis-selection from neighboring items. Checkboxes typically sit to the left of the text, though right placement is allowed for layout or spacing reasons.

side.title: Side Panel Filter (Statistics, Defects)
side.desc: Used as a filter in a Data Table to surface only the data the user wants.

task.title: Task Settings Farm Selection
task.desc: Used to multi-select items with the same characteristic.

sync.title: Sync User name Data
sync.desc: Used standalone to toggle data sync on or off.

multiple.title: Multiple Select Option
multiple.text: In a Checkbox Group with multiple items visible, one or more items can be selected (Checked).

arrangement.title: Right and Left Arrangement
arrangement.text: When grouping multiple items, place the Checkbox on the left to align the text labels consistently. Right placement applies when the Checkbox isn't part of an item list — for example, a standalone optional filter that triggers a data load.

indeterminate.title: Indeterminate State
indeterminate.text: The Indeterminate state applies when sub-items mix Checked and Unchecked. The parent reads Checked when all sub-items are Checked, and Unchecked when all sub-items are Unchecked.

radio.title: Radio Button
radio.desc: Radio Button is used when two or more options are mutually exclusive and only one can be selected. Note: one option must be selected by default.

state.title: State
state.desc: Radio Button states split by Selected / Unselected into Default, Active, Disabled. Active behaves as Hover — user-facing rest states are Default and Disabled.

size.title: Size
size.desc: Two sizes — Small and Medium. Size pairs with the Label's font size; default is 16 × 16. Small is allowed when hierarchy between elements requires it.

spacing.title: Control and Label Spacing
spacing.text: Radio Button uses 8px between the control icon and the label. List items maintain 12px between options to avoid mis-selection. The Radio icon defaults to the left of the label; right placement is allowed for layout or spacing reasons.

exactlyOne.title: Select Exactly One
exactlyOne.text: Within a single Radio Button Group, only one Radio Button can be in the Selection (Checked) state.

mutuallyExclusive.title: Mutually Exclusive Options
mutuallyExclusive.text: Radio Buttons present mutually exclusive options for single-select. The label content inside the Radio Button item list should also be mutually exclusive.

toggle.title: Toggle-Switch
toggle.desc: Toggle Switch is used when binary, mutually exclusive options require an immediate effect change.

state.title: State
state.desc: Three states — Default, Active, Disabled — each holding Checked / Unchecked (On/Off).

size.title: Size
size.desc: Two sizes — Small and Medium — chosen by the Label's hierarchy and size. Even with the same Label font size, hierarchy may shift the Toggle size.

spacing.title: Control and Label Spacing
spacing.text: Typically placed at the right of a list. Right padding shifts by the container's area and size — Medium uses 16px, Small uses 12px.

management.title: Management View Settings
management.desc: Toggle each setting on or off within View Settings.

filter.title: Filter Settings (Statistics)
filter.desc: Toggle table columns on or off, per item.

tableSettings.title: Table Settings (Defects)
tableSettings.desc: Toggle table columns on or off, per item.

withLabel.title: Toggle Switch with Label
withLabel.text: Toggles typically pair with a label describing their role. Placement (left or right) depends on the surrounding container — right placement is the convention.

group.title: Toggle Switch Group
group.text: Toggle Switches handle On/Off for their target area. When grouped, the surrounding items belong to the same hierarchy as a coherent set.
